eligibility.text: . Sign a written Informed Consent Form (ICF) and be able to comply with the visits and related procedures stipulated in the protocol
eligibility.justification: Ensuring patient understanding of study protocols minimizes potential risks associated with treatment.

eligibility.text: . Age between 18 and 75 years old
eligibility.justifica\u00e7\u00e3o: A larger sample size is needed to detect statistically significant differences in efficacy or safety.

eligibility.text: . Histologically confirmed rectal adenocarcinoma
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study focuses on a specific and well-defined condition, minimizing potential harm from diverse conditions.

eligibility.text: . According to the AJCC 8th Edition staging, imaging evaluation (enhanced CT or enhanced MRI) confirms resectable locally advanced rectal cancer (AJCC 8th Edition staging cT3-4 / cN+)
eligibility.justification: A robust and accurate diagnostic process is crucial for determining treatment efficacy.

eligibility.text: . Patients with microsatellite stability (MSS) or proficient mismatch repair (pMMR) rectal cancer
eligibility.justifica\u00e7\u00e3o: This criterion reduces the risk of adverse events by excluding patients with potentially unstable DNA.

eligibility.text: . At least one evaluable lesion according to RECIST v1.1 criteria
eligibility.justification: The strict inclusion/exclusion criteria are essential for data quality and consistency.

eligibility.text: . Liver function: Total Bilirubin (TBIL) ≤1.5×Upper Limit of Normal (ULN); Alanine transaminase (ALT) and Aspartate transamine (AST) ≤3×ULN
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures data quality by limiting the potential for errors in patient assessment.

eligibility.text: . Serum albumin (ALB) ≥35 g/L.
eligibility.justifica\u00e7\u00e3o: Ensuring adequate protein levels is crucial for maintaining patient health and preventing complications.

eligibility.text: . Hemoglobin (HGB) ≥10.0 g/dL.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study has a sufficient number of patients to achieve statistically significant results.

eligibility.text: . Blood count: Absolute Neutrophil Count (ANC) ≥1.5×10^9/L; Platelet (PLT) ≥100×10^9/L; Hemoglobin (HGB) ≥10.0 g/dL.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study has a sufficient number of patients to achieve statistically significant results.

eligibility.text: .  Liver function: Total Bilirubin (TBIL) ≤1.5×Upper Limit of Normal (ULN); Alanine transaminase (ALT) and Aspartate transamine (AST) ≤3×ULN
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures data quality by limiting the potential for errors in patient assessment.

eligibility.text: .  Kidney function: Serum creatinine ≤1.5×ULN or creatinine clearance ≥50 ml/min (calculated using the Cockcroft-Gault formula or standard 24-hour urine collection method); Urine dipstick test shows urine protein <2+; For subjects with baseline urine dipstick test showing urine protein ≥2+, a 24-hour urine collection should be performed, and the protein content in the 24-hour urine should be <1 g.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures data quality by limiting the potential for errors in patient assessment.

eligibility.text: .  No serious concomitant diseases that threaten the subject's survival (resulting in an expected survival time of less than 5 years)
eligibility.justification: This criterion minimizes risks to patients by excluding conditions that could significantly shorten their lifespan.

eligibility.text: .  Female subjects of childbearing potential or male subjects with partners of childbearing potential must use effective contraception throughout the treatment period. Female subjects must have evidence of postmenopausal status or a negative urine or serum pregnancy test result for premenopausal female subjects.
eligibility.justification: This criterion protects patients from unintended pregnancies and ensures responsible data collection.

eligibility.text: . Patients with rectal cancer who have been tested for microsatellite instability as MSI-H or mismatch repair as dMMR
eligibility.justification: This criterion reduces the risk of adverse events by excluding patients with potentially unstable DNA.

eligibility.text: . Patients who have previously received any anti-tumor treatment for the studied disease, including surgery, radiotherapy, chemotherapy, targeted therapy, immunotherapy, etc.
eligibility.justification: This criterion minimizes potential risks associated with multiple treatments.

eligibility.text: . Simultaneous participation in another clinical study, unless participating in an observational (non-interventional) clinical study or in the survival follow-up phase of an interventional study
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures data integrity by preventing duplication of effort.

eligibility.text: . Treatment with any investigational drug or device within 4 weeks prior to the first dose of the study drug
eligibility.justifica\u00e7\u00e3o: This criterion minimizes potential risks associated with unexpected adverse events.

eligibility.text: . Receipt of live or attenuated live vaccines within 4 weeks prior to the first dose of the study drug, excluding: intranasal inhaled local steroid therapy or local steroid injections (such as intra-articular injections); systemic corticosteroid therapy not exceeding 10 mg/day of prednisone or its equivalent physiological dose; glucocorticoids as preventive medication for allergic reactions (such as pre-CT medication); use of Chinese herbal medicine or immunomodulatory drugs with anti-tumor indications (including thymosin, interferon, interleukin, etc.) within 1 week prior to the first dose of the study drug
eligibility.justification: This criterion minimizes potential risks associated with unexpected adverse events.

eligibility.text: .  Use of immunosuppressive drugs within 4 weeks prior to the first dose of the study drug, excluding: intranasal inhaled local steroid therapy or local steroid injections (such as intra-articular injections); systemic corticosteroid therapy not exceeding 10 mg/day of prednisone or its equivalent physiological dose; glucocorticoids as preventive medication for allergic reactions (such as pre-CT medication); use of Chinese herbal medicine or immunomodulatory drugs with anti-tumor indications (including thymosin, interferon, interleukin, etc.) within 1 week prior to the first dose of the study drug
eligibility.justification: This criterion minimizes potential risks associated with unexpected adverse events.

eligibility.text: .  History of blood transfusion within 14 days before the first dose of the study drug or expected during the study period.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures data quality by limiting the potential for errors in patient assessment.

eligibility.text: .  Patient with rectal cancer who have been tested for microsatellite instability as MSI-H or mismatch repair as dMMR
eligibility.justification: This criterion reduces the risk of adverse events by excluding patients with potentially unstable DNA.

eligibility.justification: This criterion reduces the risk of adverse events by excluding patients with potentially unstable DNA.
